title: GitHub - monto-fe/smtp-probe: Open-source SMTP deliverability diagnostic tool — SPF/DKIM/DMARC checks, SMTP verification & RBL blacklist scanning
excerpt: Open-source SMTP deliverability diagnostic tool — SPF/DKIM/DMARC checks, SMTP verification & RBL blacklist scanning - monto-fe/smtp-probe
content_html:
中文文档

Powered by uSpeedo — production-ready global email delivery API.

  • Email: support@mail-uspeedo.com
  • WhatsApp: +52 8123200311
  • Telegram: @uSpeedo_Admin

An open-source email deliverability diagnostic tool. Check SPF/DKIM/DMARC configuration, verify email addresses via SMTP, and scan IPs against 29 RBL blacklists — all from a single CLI or HTTP API.

Features

  • Domain Health Check — SPF, DKIM, DMARC, MX, PTR records with 0–100 scoring
  • SMTP Email Verification — RCPT TO check via MX servers with bind-IP support
  • IP Pool Management — Multiple bind IPs with health checks and automatic failover
  • Multi-Node Coordination — GeoIP-based routing across distributed nodes
  • Disposable Email Detection — Detect temporary email providers with 4 upstream data sources (~8k–74k domains each)
  • RBL Blacklist Scan — Check IPs against 29 blacklists (Spamhaus, Barracuda, SpamCop, etc.)
  • Latency Probing — Measure connection latency from each pool IP to target domains
  • HTTP API — RESTful JSON API for programmatic integration
  • Multiple Output Formats — text (CLI table), JSON, Markdown

Installation

Quick Install (Recommended)

bash <(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/monto-fe/smtp-probe/main/install.sh)

Or specify a version:

VERSION=v0.1.0 bash <(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/monto-fe/smtp-probe/main/install.sh)

From Source

go install github.com/monto-fe/smtp-probe@latest

Usage

Check Domain Configuration

smtp-probe check gmail.com
smtp-probe check example.com

Verify Email Address (requires outbound port 25)

smtp-probe verify user@example.com
smtp-probe verify user@example.com --bind-ip 1.2.3.4 --timeout 30s

Note

SMTP verification requires outbound access to port 25, which is commonly blocked by ISPs and cloud providers. The check command (DNS-based) works without port 25 and covers SPF/DKIM/DMARC/MX/PTR diagnostics.

Verify with Disposable Email Detection

smtp-probe verify user@mailinator.com --disposable

On first use with --disposable, the default blocklist (martenson, ~8k domains, CC0) is downloaded automatically. If the email domain is disposable, SMTP verification is skipped.

Verify with IP Pool and Automatic Failover

# Add IPs to pool
smtp-probe pool add 1.2.3.4 --domain mail-a.example.com
smtp-probe pool add 1.2.3.5 --domain mail-b.example.com

# Verify with pool (auto-failover on blacklist/timeout)
smtp-probe verify user@example.com --pool

# Check pool health
smtp-probe pool list
smtp-probe pool check

When --pool is used, smtp-probe selects a healthy IP from the pool. If an IP gets blacklisted (5xx response), it's automatically marked and the next healthy IP is tried (up to 3 retries).

Measure SMTP Latency

# Single probe from default outbound IP
smtp-probe probe gmail.com

# Probe from each IP in the pool
smtp-probe probe gmail.com --pool

Multi-Node Deployment

Deploy multiple smtp-probe servers across different regions and coordinate them:

# Start node A in Singapore
smtp-probe serve --port 8080 \
  --node-id sg-01 --region asia-southeast1 \
  --lat 1.35 --lon 103.82 \
  --peers http://us-node:8080,http://eu-node:8080

# Start node B in US West
smtp-probe serve --port 8080 \
  --node-id us-01 --region us-west1 \
  --lat 37.77 --lon -122.42 \
  --peers http://sg-node:8080,http://eu-node:8080

Each node automatically:

  • Registers with all peers via heartbeat (every 30s)
  • Routes verification requests to the geographically closest node (based on MX server location)
  • Falls back to local execution if remote node is unavailable

Check IP Against Blacklists

smtp-probe blacklist 1.2.3.4

Generate Full Report

smtp-probe report example.com
smtp-probe report example.com --format json
smtp-probe report example.com --format md > report.md

Start HTTP Server

smtp-probe serve
smtp-probe serve --port 9090
# Multi-node with peers:
smtp-probe serve --port 8080 --node-id node-01 --peers http://10.0.0.2:8080

HTTP API

Endpoint Method Parameters
/v1/health GET —
/v1/check GET domain=example.com
/v1/verify GET email=user@example.com, bind_ip=1.2.3.4, timeout=60s, disposable=true, pool=true
/v1/blacklist GET ip=1.2.3.4
/v1/report GET domain=example.com
/v1/peer/register POST {node_id, addr, region, lat, lon, pool_size} (multi-node)
/v1/peer/peers GET — (list registered peers)
/v1/peer/heartbeat POST {node_id, ...} (multi-node heartbeat)
/v1/peer/geo GET ip=1.2.3.4 (GeoIP lookup) 
curl http://localhost:8080/v1/check?domain=gmail.com
curl "http://localhost:8080/v1/verify?email=user@example.com"
curl http://localhost:8080/v1/blacklist?ip=1.2.3.4

Scoring System

Check Deduction
No SPF -20
Invalid SPF -10
No DKIM -15
No DMARC -20
Weak DMARC (p=none) -10
No MX -30

Architecture

smtp-probe/
├── main.go                  # Entry point
├── cmd/                     # Cobra CLI commands
├── pkg/                     # Core packages
│   ├── probe/               # Orchestrator and scoring
│   ├── dnscheck/            # DNS record checks (SPF/DKIM/DMARC/MX/PTR)
│   ├── disposable/          # Disposable email domain detection
│   ├── pool/                # IP pool management and health checks
│   ├── node/                # Multi-node coordination (GeoIP, routing, heartbeat)
│   ├── rbl/                 # RBL blacklist queries
│   ├── smtpclient/          # SMTP RCPT TO verification
│   └── types/               # Shared type definitions
└── server/                  # HTTP API (Gin)

Configuration

Verification Flags

Flag Default Description
--bind-ip (outbound IP) Source IP for SMTP connections
--timeout 60s SMTP/DNS timeout
--port 8080 HTTP server port
--format text Output format: json, md, text
--disposable false Enable disposable email provider check
--pool false Use IP pool with automatic failover

IP Pool Management

# Add IP to pool
smtp-probe pool add 1.2.3.4 --domain mail-a.example.com

# Remove IP from pool
smtp-probe pool remove 1.2.3.4

# List all IPs and their status
smtp-probe pool list

# Check health of all IPs (RBL scan)
smtp-probe pool check

# Check health of a specific IP
smtp-probe pool check 1.2.3.4

Pool is persisted at ~/.smtp-probe/pool.json.

Multi-Node Flags

Flag Default Description
--node-id (auto) Unique identifier for this node
--region — Region name (e.g., asia-southeast1, us-west1)
--lat 0 Node latitude
--lon 0 Node longitude
--peers — Initial peer addresses (comma-separated)
--heartbeat 30s Heartbeat interval for peer nodes

Managing Disposable Email Lists

# List all sources and their status
smtp-probe disposable list

# Download specific sources
smtp-probe disposable download disposable fakefilter

# Download all available sources
smtp-probe disposable download --all

# Update stale lists (re-downloads if older than 7 days)
smtp-probe disposable update

# Install daily auto-update via crontab
smtp-probe disposable update --install-cron
Source Domains Default Description
martenson ~8k Yes CC0 blocklist used by PyPI
disposable ~74k No Multi-source aggregation
tompec ~20k No Successor to ivolo (suffix mode)
fakefilter ~10k No Automated monitoring

Development

git clone https://github.com/monto-fe/smtp-probe.git
cd smtp-probe
go build -o smtp-probe .
go test ./...

Contributing

  1. Fork the repository
  2. Create your feature branch (git checkout -b feature/amazing)
  3. Run tests: go test ./...
  4. Run linter: golangci-lint run
  5. Commit your changes
  6. Open a Pull Request

License

MIT — see LICENSE
